description: Evaluate the feasibility of introducing an engineered disulfide bond into wild-type T4 lysozyme (PDB: 2LZM) to increase its thermal stability. Load the structure and measure the specific distance between the C-beta (CB) atom of Isoleucine 3 and the C-beta (CB) atom of Cysteine 97 to verify it falls within the permissible range for disulfide formation. Create a high-quality visualization showing the protein with these two residues highlighted in stick representation and a dashed line showing the specific CB-CB measurement. Save the rendered figure to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/t4l_engineered_disulfide.png. Additionally, write a geometric analysis report to ~/PyMOL_Data/t4l_disulfide_report.txt explicitly mentioning the two target residues (ILE 3 and CYS 97), reporting the exact measured C-beta to C-beta distance in Angstroms, and concluding whether the distance is geometrically favorable.
